summary: Current Discover doctrine and MVP execution state after ontology, rail, and roadmap cleanup.
locks: Home is the left destination, Discover is the right destination, and Plot lives inside Home.
Discover is player-anchored and inherits the active listening scope rather than creating a parallel context system.
Sources are followable origin objects; signals are the objects users receive, add, and act on.
Recommendations are explicit user-to-signal relationships rendered through the avatar + balloon grammar.
Support is a lightweight post-level counter/reaction, not a separate authored post type.
Discover uses one top search bar, then Popular Singles, then Recommendations, then the persistent player with Travel attached below.
Popular Singles uses the MVP lenses Most Added, Supported Now, and Recent Rises.
Popular Now remains owned by RaDIYo/broadcast momentum logic and is not a Discover lens.
Community visit is a secondary Discover outcome, not the primary Discover success condition.
open_decisions: Define the exact implementation rules for Most Added, Supported Now, and Recent Rises in apps/web.
Finish the founder pass on Home / Feed / user profile.
Lock the broader statistics contract that community and Discover surfaces will read from without finalizing the full Activity Points system.
drift_corrections: Do not treat Plot as a peer destination to Home.
Do not treat recommendations as freeform user-written text.
Do not assume venues or ambassadors are MVP systems.
Do not treat all signals as equally relevant in Discover; promos and events are conditional content.
Do not reuse RaDIYo's Popular Now momentum semantics inside Discover.
next_actions: Finish Home / Feed / user profile founder pass.
Translate the Discover founder lock into an apps/web implementation plan.
Reconcile any stale Discover prompt packs or solution docs that still use High Activity or generic Popular Signals wording.
